title: Pan-Seared Canadian Duck Breast with Juicy Stew
description: A rich and savory dish featuring seared duck breast served with a tangy stew made with the same meat. Perfect for a special occasion or a cozy night in.
instructions: Season the duck breasts with salt and black pepper.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat and sear the duck breasts for 3-4 minutes per side.
Transfer the duck to the oven and cook for 10-12 minutes or until the internal temperature reaches 130°F (54°C) for medium-rare.
While the duck is cooking, prepare the stew by browning the beef in a separate skillet.
Add the onion and garlic to the skillet and cook until softened.
Add the stew cherries, carrots, and flour to the skillet and stir to combine.
Cover the skillet and simmer the stew for 10-12 minutes or until the carrots are tender.
Serve the seared duck breast with the stew and garnish with fresh herbs if desired.
